title: Pedilonum vexillarius
taxonomy: Pedilonum vexillarius is a species of plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is classified as a flowering plant, specifically within the order Gentianales. The plant is also classified as being in the family Rubiaceae and the genus Pedilonum. Pedilonum vexillarius is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Its classification is further specified by its scientific name, which is used to identify the species within the Rubiaceae family.
uses: Pedilonum vexillarius, also known as the velvet leaf, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant's leaves are said to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in treating wounds and infections. The plant's medicinal uses are primarily based on traditional knowledge and have not been extensively scientifically studied.
